title: Level 631
wikitext:
{{DISPLAYTITLE:Level 631: "The Backwaters"}}__NOTOC__ {{Class 5|class=Undetermined|hexagon=?|safety=Mysterious Properties|security=Unknown Information|entity=Undocumented Entities|color-h=130|color-s=25%|color-l=50%}} '''The Backwaters''' is a highly unexplored level due to its scarcity of entry. It is considered by many to be a deadzone due to the many disappearances that occur within it. {{Blurimage|file=Backwaters.jpg|caption=An image of a path in Level 631.}} ==Description== '''Level 631''', better known as '''The Backwaters''', bears the likeness of a perpetually foggy swamp. Rivers and lakes are common yet stagnant and bodies of water have always been reported to end somewhere after extended exploration, indicating that each body of water is ultimately isolated from one another no matter how many rivers each one has. The resulting microbe infestations seem far from fatal, but exposure to them can still cause common symptoms of sickness, as well as hallucinations. These delusions are, for whatever reason, of very specific nature; those experiencing them see shapes moving within the fog and mud and hear the sound of crickets rising and falling at random intervals, sometimes to the point of being deafening. The level is well illuminated yet noticeably darker than even an overcast landscape during the day. Obscured and possibly also dimmed by the fog is an evident overhead source of light — possibly celestial. The cold temperature always appears to stay the same; season changes or even natural phenomena such as rain or lightning have never been reported. Meanwhile, the plants within the marsh possess weak foliage but show no signs of expiring, seemingly only requiring the moisture in the ground and possibly even the air to survive. Strangely, branches and even trees that have fallen are common, but these have still shown surprising signs of life. The species of any of the plant life in '''Level 631''' are unknown and thought to be exclusive to the level. {{Blurimage|file=Lair.jpg|caption=A photograph that was found in '''Level 631'''. "Lair" was written on the back.}} The ground of the swamp is sticky and greatly inhibits the movement of any who walk upon it. Moreover, it is wet and soft enough that standing in one place for too long will end with one slowly sinking into the mud. Failing to escape within the first few seconds of such a descent makes it nigh impossible to do so afterward, dooming one to suffocate within the mire. Thus, resting on the mud or against trees is extremely dangerous. The ground's composition remains the same even underneath rivers and lakes, albeit without posing as much of a hazard there due to the water. Branching irregularly and seemingly without pattern throughout the swamp are slightly suspended wooden paths that intersect and end just as randomly. They are the most ideal method of traversal considering the hazardous nature of the surrounding mire. Moreover, they exhibit no signs of rot and are completely stable. The only downside is their unpredictable routes. Disappearances in the level are unnervingly common, so much so that entry is considered a death sentence even without the matter of the lack of exits. Even whole groups have gone missing without a trace. Speculation regarding whether an undocumented type of entity is behind this is frequent and research is scheduled to determine the true cause. It is expected that some answers will be gained once an experienced documentation squad with recording equipment is deployed via [[The Hub]], but the level's associated [[Object 2|Key]] has not yet been found. ==Entities== While no entities have been documented in '''Level 631''', wanderers within have rarely claimed to spot ripples in the water, as if something were moving under the surface. Whatever this is is presumed to be some kind of entity, possibly serving as an explanation for the mysterious disappearances in the level. ==Colonies & Outposts== Human settlements of any kind cannot exist in '''Level 631'''. Something in the level, entity or not, is preventing sustained human inhabitation. ==Entrances & Exits== ===Entrances=== * One has an extremely rare chance of entering the Backwaters after falling through The Void for longer than the standard noclip time. ===Exits=== * No exits to the Backwaters have been discovered and it is unknown if any even exist. {{Collapse|num=1|initial=[[File:Eyecon.png|90px|center|link=]]|final=|body=
[[File:Old Parchment.png|700px|center|frameless]]
[[File:Wendiskull.png|175px|link=]]
The most horrific thing stalks the rancid swamp. Its head looks to be a deer skull, but its body is said to be anything but natural. Grey flesh holding human-looking bones as a mire does a sinking soul; numerous clawed limbs that extend from a corpselike body. This creature almost seems to appear out of thin air whenever it attacks. Some claim to have seen it rising from the mud like a grotesque revenant, claiming victims before they even have time to utter a single shriek. Cherish those mazes of madness with no monstrous threats on the horizon, for some of these realms have their minotaur.
